betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Satellites§f

§aLoft expensive orbital platforms on Soyuz rockets — scan resources, map terrain, and unlock advanced detection!§f

Satellites are expensive items launched into space on §bSoyuz rockets§f to provide various services. Multiple satellite types exist, each with different functions and recipes. Satellites must be properly linked before launch using a §bSatellite ID Manager§f, otherwise they become useless and unrecoverable.

§6Linking§f (Satellite ID Manager):
§b• Left Slot§f: Source ID for copying.
§b• Middle Slot§f: Destination for copied IDs.
§b• Right Slot§f: Generates a random ID.
Place a satellite in the randomizing slot, then copy its ID to an interface or chip. If two satellites share the same ID, the older one becomes useless.

§6Launching§f: Requires a §bSoyuz Launcher§f with a Soyuz rocket (§e128,000mB§f Kerosene per launch). The satellite needs a Satellite Control Interface with matching frequency.

§6Satellite Base§f (Assembly Factory — required for all satellite crafting):
§b•§f Rubber Bar ×12, Titanium Shell ×3, Medium Thruster, Low-Density Element ×8
§b•§f Desh Compound Plate ×4, Kerosene Barrel, Photovoltaic Panel ×24
§b•§f Integrated Circuit Board ×12, Lithium-Ion Battery

§6Satellite Types§f:
§b• Depth-Resource Scanning Module§f: Created by combining an M700 Survey Scanner with a Satellite Base in the Arc Welder. Creates a top-down map of underground ores — use with the Neutrino Lens for maximum effectiveness.

§eTip§f: Satellites are a significant investment. Build your Soyuz infrastructure first (launcher, fuel production, control interface), then craft the satellite. The §bM700 Survey Scanner§f (3 Steel Shells + 8 Cast Titanium Plates + Desh + Magnetrons + Military Grade Circuits) must be crafted separately before assembly!
